journal_entry.reason: Upon signing the lease, Mondelez received a $25,000 security deposit in cash. A security deposit is a refundable amount held as collateral and creates a liability until it is either refunded to the tenant or applied against damages/unpaid rent at lease end. No revenue is recognized at this point because the deposit is not earned income—it is a conditional obligation. The monthly rent of $10,000 will be recognized as rental income when earned over the lease term, but no rent payment has been received or earned yet in this transaction.

output_debit_classifier.reason: Mondelez leased a portion of its building and received a $25,000 security deposit in cash upon lease signing. Under IFRS, a security deposit is not revenue—it is a refundable liability that will be settled at the end of the lease term (either refunded to the tenant or applied against damages/unpaid obligations). The transaction records the receipt of cash and the corresponding liability. The monthly rent of $10,000 will be recognized as rental income when earned over the lease term, but this transaction only covers the initial security deposit receipt. No tax is mentioned in the transaction text, so no tax lines are included.
